metadata.seed: I keep overthinking everything at work, and I'm scared I'm going to get fired even though nobody said anything.

patient.query: I keep overthinking everything at work, and I'm scared I'm going to get fired even though nobody said anything.

automatic_thoughts: I'm going to get fired
nobody said anything

entailment: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: You seem to be feeling quite anxious about your work, and it's understandable that you might worry about making mistakes. However, it's important to remember that everyone makes errors sometimes. Let's explore together how we can approach this situation differently.

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I do feel anxious about it. I just... I worry that if I make a mistake, it’ll really reflect poorly on me, you know? Like, what if my boss thinks I’m not capable? It just kind of hangs over me all the time.

automatic_thoughts: if I make a mistake, it’ll really reflect poorly on me
what if my boss thinks I’m not capable

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: I understand that you're feeling anxious, and it seems like this anxiety stems from concerns about making mistakes and how others might perceive your capabilities. Let's explore these thoughts together and see if we can find a way to alleviate some of this stress.

patient.query: Yeah, that sounds okay. I just keep thinking about all the times I’ve messed up and how people reacted. It feels like I’m constantly on edge, waiting for that reaction again. I don’t want to be seen as a failure, but then I also feel like I can’t live up to everyone’s expectations.

automatic_thoughts: I’ve messed up
people reacted
I’m constantly on edge
I don’t want to be seen as a failure
I can’t live up to everyone’s expectations

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: You've had some challenging experiences, and it's understandable to feel anxious about making mistakes. It seems like you're putting a lot of pressure on yourself to meet others' expectations. Let's explore together if these expectations are realistic and how they might be impacting your feelings.

patient.query: I guess it is a lot of pressure, isn’t it? I sometimes feel like I have to be perfect, and if I’m not, I’ll disappoint everyone. It’s just hard to shake off that feeling, especially when I think about how others might see me. I want to meet those expectations, but it feels like an impossible task.

triggers: thinking about how others might see me
automatic_thoughts: I have to be perfect
if I’m not, I’ll disappoint everyone
it feels like an impossible task

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're dealing with a lot of pressure and high expectations, both from yourself and others. It's understandable to feel this way when we strive for perfection. Let's explore together if these expectations are realistic and how they might be impacting your feelings.

patient.query: I mean, yeah, I guess they’re not always realistic. But sometimes it feels like if I don’t aim high, I’ll just end up failing anyway. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle of trying to prove myself, but then I’m terrified of falling short. I don’t know how to just… let go and not worry about what others think.

automatic_thoughts: if I don’t aim high, I’ll just end up failing anyway
I’m stuck in this cycle of trying to prove myself
I’m terrified of falling short
I don’t know how to just let go and not worry about what others think

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're setting high standards for yourself, and it can be challenging when things don't go as planned. It's important to remember that everyone makes mistakes, and they're opportunities for learning rather than signs of failure. Let's explore ways we can help you manage your expectations and reduce the fear of falling short.

patient.query: I get that, but it’s just hard for me to see mistakes as anything other than failures. I don't know, it feels like if I let my guard down or lower my standards, I might just… stop trying altogether. I want to feel okay with making mistakes, but right now it just feels too risky.

automatic_thoughts: mistakes are failures
letting my guard down means stopping trying
making mistakes feels too risky

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're struggling with the idea that making mistakes doesn't necessarily mean failure. It's understandable to feel this way, but let's explore together if there might be other ways to view your efforts and progress.

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: letting my guard down means stopping trying
retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

patient.query: I guess it makes sense to think about it that way, but still, I just can’t shake that knot in my stomach when I think about screwing up. Like, it feels so real and heavy. I want to believe it’s okay to mess up, but part of me is terrified of what that would mean for me. It’s hard to relax into that idea.

automatic_thoughts: I can't shake that knot in my stomach
it feels so real and heavy
I'm terrified of what that would mean for me
behaviors: hard to relax into that idea

neutral: Unable to comprehend concept of danger (finding)
Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a heavy burden when you think about making a mistake. It's understandable to feel anxious, but it might be helpful to explore what exactly makes this fear so intense for you. Let's delve deeper into your thoughts and feelings around this situation.

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely feels heavy. I think part of it is just this fear of judgment, you know? Like, if I mess up, I’m worried people will see me as weak or not good enough. It just makes me feel... small, like I’m not worth their time or respect. I never want to feel that way.

automatic_thoughts: if I mess up, people will see me as weak
I'm not good enough
I'm not worth their time or respect

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: I understand how you're feeling, and it's okay to have those thoughts. Many people struggle with feelings of self-doubt and fear of judgment. Let's explore together if there might be other ways to interpret these situations that could help you feel more confident and valued.

patient.query: I appreciate you saying that, but it’s just hard for me to imagine things being different. I mean, the fear feels so ingrained. Sometimes, I think about all the times I’ve been judged or criticized, and it just sticks with me. I don’t know if I can really change how I see it, you know? It feels like I’m always going to carry that weight.

automatic_thoughts: it’s hard for me to imagine things being different
the fear feels so ingrained
I can’t really change how I see it
I’m always going to carry that weight

patient.query: It’s just hard for me to imagine things being different. The fear feels so ingrained, like it’s always there. I think about all the times I’ve been judged or criticized, and it sticks with me. I don’t know if I can change how I see it; it feels like I’m always going to carry that weight.
